name: Whole Wheat Molasses Bread
recipeInstructions:
For Bread Machine: Place the ingredients in the order listed into the pan. Select white bread and choose high crust setting. Push Start. This is a very moist cake-like bread and one of our favorites. Makes 1 loaf.
Scratch Method:
1. Combine whole wheat flour, 1 cup of all-purpose (better for bread) flour, yeast, caraway seeds in a bowl.
2. In another bowl, heat water, molasses, brown sugar, shortening, and salt until warm, stirring to melt the shortening.
3. Add the liquid mixture to the dry ingredients. Mix at low speed for 30 seconds, then on high speed for 3 minutes.
4. Stir in enough of the remaining flour to make a stiff dough. Knead the dough until smooth and elastic.
5. Shape the dough into a ball and place it in a greased bowl. Let rise until doubled. To speed up rising, place the bowl with the light on in the oven.
6. Punch down the dough and divide it into two loaves.
7. Place the shaped loaves on a greased baking sheet and let rise until doubled.
8. Bake at 375°F for 30 to 35 minutes. For a chewier crust, brush the bread with warm water during the last 10 to 15 minutes of baking.
9. Remove from the sheet and cool on racks. Makes 2 loaves. I have made this bread for many years; it's almost like eating cake! My husband said he couldn't tell if I made it from scratch or in the bread machine.
